response:
<<vegetable>> The hexagonal community center has six equilateral triangles as its faces, each with a side length of 30 meters. The area of a regular hexagon can be calculated using the formula:  
$$
\text{Area} = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} \times \text{side}^2
$$  
Substituting the side length of 30 meters:  
$$
\text{Area} = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 30^2 = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 900 = 1350\sqrt{3} \text{ square meters}
$$  

<<vegetable>> The circular garden is inscribed within the hexagon, with its circumference touching the midpoints of the hexagon's sides. The radius of the garden can be calculated using the relationship between the radius of the circumscribed circle of a regular hexagon and its side length. For a regular hexagon, the radius of the circumscribed circle is equal to the side length. Therefore, the radius of the garden is 30 meters. The area of the circular garden is then:  
$$
\text{Area} = \pi \times \text{radius}^2 = \pi \times 30^2 = 900\pi \text{ square meters}
$$
